name: Abbas ibn Ali
title: Moon of Bani Hashem / Standard-bearer
shortDescription: The brave son of Imam Ali (AS) and loyal brother of Imam Hussain, the absolute symbol of sacrifice and loyalty in history.
fullStory:
Abbas ibn Ali (AS), the brave son of Amir al-Mu'minin Ali (AS) and Fatima bint Hizam (Umm ul-Banin), is one of the most prominent figures of the Bani Hashem family. His mother, who hailed from a courageous and warrior tribe, always raised her children to serve the children of Lady Fatima (SA). Due to his extremely handsome face, radiant countenance, and tall stature, Abbas (AS) was known among the Arabs as the 'Moon of Bani Hashem' and displayed unparalleled bravery from his youth.

Throughout all stages of the Ashura uprising, from Medina to Mecca and then to Karbala, he was present alongside his brother Imam Hussain (AS) like a shadow. He was entrusted with the command and the role of standard-bearer (Alamdar) of the Imam's small army. His loyalty was so profound that when Shimr ibn Dhi al-Jawshan (who shared tribal roots with Hazrat Abbas's mother) brought a letter of safe conduct for him and his brothers on the afternoon of Tasu'a to save their lives, Abbas furiously rejected it. He proclaimed: 'May God curse you and your safe conduct! Should we be safe while the son of the Prophet is not?' and steadfastly remained by the Imam's side.

In the days leading up to Ashura, when Yazid's army blocked the Imam's caravan from accessing water, Hazrat Abbas held the crucial responsibility of bringing water to the tents. For this reason, one of his titles is 'Saqqa' (the water carrier). The sounds of crying and wailing from the young children, who were restless from severe thirst, pained Abbas's kind heart and made him determined to bring water to the tents at any cost.

According to the precise report of the book Al-Irshad written by Sheikh Mufid, when thirst severely overcame Imam Hussain (AS), his companions, and the children of the camp, he personally moved towards the Euphrates River along with his brother Abbas to bring water. Seeing this, the army of Umar ibn Sa'd blocked their path and, by creating a heavy and comprehensive siege, separated Imam Hussain and Abbas to prevent them from reaching the water.

Sheikh Mufid continues in Al-Irshad: Abbas (AS), who was entirely surrounded by the massive enemy force alone, engaged in battle with unparalleled bravery and steadfastness. The enemies, lacking the strength and courage to face him directly, cowardly attacked him from all sides. After inflicting heavy casualties on the Kufan army, he was eventually martyred by Zayd ibn Riqad al-Juhani and Hakim ibn Tufayl al-Ta'i, who were lying in ambush. Imam Hussain (AS) was deeply moved upon seeing the blood-soaked body of his brother and wept bitterly over the loss of his main supporter.
quoteArabic: وَاللَّهِ إِنْ قَطَعْتُمْ یَمیني، إِنّي أُحامي أَبَداً عَنْ دیني
quote: By God, even if you cut off my right hand, I will forever defend my religion and an Imam who is the son of the Prophet.
